description: Node/TypeScript ecosystem groupings — peer-dep coupled families that must move together.

toolSettings.description: Cap (MB) for the pnpm/yarn child process Renovate spawns to refresh lockfiles — the usual OOM culprit on Mend-hosted, where NODE_OPTIONS can't be set. 2560 = 2.5GiB; Mend imposes no max beyond the plan, so raise toward that ceiling if runs still OOM. Requires renovate ≥ the version pinned in validate.yml. https://docs.renovatebot.com/mend-hosted/faq/

packageRules.description: Vite + plugins (plugin majors track Vite majors)
Vitest stack (engine + coverage + testcontainers + vite peer)
ESLint plugins + typescript-eslint. The bare `typescript-eslint` meta-package is unscoped, so the /^@typescript-eslint// regex never matched it — it must be listed explicitly.

TypeScript compiler family — TypeScript 7 (the Go port) ships no JS API, so tools needing programmatic compiler access (typescript-eslint, Next.js) require the `@typescript/typescript6` compat package aliased over the `typescript` specifier while a second alias supplies the v7 `tsc` bin. Group them so the side-by-side aliases move coherently. https://github.com/typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint/issues/10940 — see also the "Running side-by-side with TypeScript 6.0" section of https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-7-0/
Prisma — client + adapter + CLI must bump together
Cloudflare Workers toolchain — wrangler + workers-types + workers SDKs travel together
Node + pnpm toolchain — engines, packageManager, .nvmrc, Dockerfile FROM all move in lockstep

Per-LTS-lane Node slots — workflow matrix entries annotated with `depName=node-lts-NN packageName=node` represent a specific LTS line and must not be major-bumped. Listed lanes cover current + near-future LTSs; add a new entry when a new LTS ships.
